buyGbit.title: How to Buy crypto on GbitX
buyGbit.s1: Create an Account on GbitX. After a successful Sign Up, Log into your account.
buyGbit.s2: Select Buy/Sell by tapping on the menu bar and select Buy from the page that popups
buyGbit.s3: Fill all the fields provided to make a Buy order. example (crypto, Network, Address, payment method, amount and quantity etc.)
buyGbit.s4: Make sure the crypto Network and the Address are correct to avoid asset loss and tap on Buy
buyGbit.s5: You will be redirected to the orders page and select the pending order you made by tapping on it.
buyGbit.s6: Tap on Chat to move to the chat box and get the payment details and make payment to the payment method provided
buyGbit.s7: Now wait for the crypto to reflect in your address wallet

sellGbit.title: How to Sell crypto on GbitX
sellGbit.s1: Create an Account on GbitX. After a successful Sign Up, Log into your account.
sellGbit.s2: Select 'Buy/Sell' by tapping on the menu bar and select Sell from the page that popups
sellGbit.s3: Provide all the details to the forms provide (crypto, Network, payment method, amount and quantity)
sellGbit.s4: Make sure the crypto Network provided is what you send the asset through
sellGbit.s5: Make sure you send the correct amount to avoid delay in payment and Tap on Sell
sellGbit.s6: You will be redirected to the orders page and select the pending order you made by tapping on it.
sellGbit.s7: Tap on 'Chat' to move to the chat box to get the Network details and send the crypto
sellGbit.s8: Now wait for the payment to reflect in your account balance

cryptoNetwork.title: How to get your crypto Network and corresponding Address (Buy Crypto)
cryptoNetwork.s1: Open your crypto wallet (Binance, OKX, coinbase, Trust Wallet, Bybit, Gate.io, BC Games, Meta Mask and any other crypto wallet)
cryptoNetwork.s4Note: Note: Choose a fast network with no fees if any
cryptoNetwork.s5: Copy the Address in the page
cryptoNetwork.s6: Now on the GbitX buy section, select the Network you selected in your crypto wallet
cryptoNetwork.s7: Paste the Address in the "Crypto Address" field
cryptoNetwork.s8: Fill in all required form fields and continue to buy crypto

gbitSend.title: How to send crypto to GbitX address (Sell crypto)
gbitSend.s1: Open your crypto wallet (Binance, OKX, coinbase, Trust Wallet, Bybit, Gate.io, BC Games, Meta Mask and any other crypto wallet)
gbitSend.s3: Select the crypto you want to Sell
gbitSend.s5Pre: Choose the Network that you selected on GbitX sell.
gbitSend.s5Note: Note: Choose a fast network with less fees if any
gbitSend.s6: Copy the GbitX Address in the order chat system and paste it in the Address field
gbitSend.s7: Enter the correct Amount and Submit
gbitSend.s8: Make sure the order is complete and successful
gbitSend.s9: Copy the Transaction ID and paste it in the order chat system
gbitSend.s10: Wait patiently for payment to reflect in your account balance

gbitApprovals.title: How to Approve to payment MTN(Subscriber, Agent, Merchant)
gbitApprovals.subtitle: Agent
gbitApprovals.s6: Choose the correct Agent name with the correct corresponding Amount to buy.

sendMoney.title: How to Send Money to Ghana through Crypto
sendMoney.s1Pre: Sign up for GbitX and verify your email.
sendMoney.s2: Sign up for a crypto wallet such as Binance, OKX, Coinbase, Bybit etc.
sendMoney.s3: Make sure you provide all the required details to verify your account on that crypto Wallet
sendMoney.s4: Now, buy crypto (USDT, BTC) in the amount you want to send to Ghana eg. ($50) using the payment method in the country you are available at the moment.
sendMoney.s5: After receiving the Crypto, Log in to your verified GbitX account and navigate to the 'Buy/Sell'
sendMoney.s6: Select Sell and fill in the type of crypto and its corresponding Network as well as the quantity of crypto to send
sendMoney.s7: Fill in the Payment Method of the receiver, such as Mobile Money or Bank Transfer.
sendMoney.s8: Now select sell and open the Chat of that order to check the details to send the correct Amount of crypto to
sendMoney.s9: After Sending crypto notify Agent in the chat with the transaction ID and wait for the Money to be sent to the receiver
sendMoney.s10: Receipt of Payment will be sent to your Email for you to verify

installIos.title: How to Install GbitX on Iphone/ IOS
installIos.s2: Go to Gbitcrypto.com and login into your account.
installIos.s3: Tap the share button at the bottom and choose "Add to Home Screen".
installIos.s4: Tap Add to confirm. The GbitX icon will now be on your Home Screen just like the App.

learn.faqSubtitle: Find answers to common questions about using GbitX

about.title: About Us
about.subtitle: Crypto made simple for Africa and beyond
about.heroSubtitle: GbitX is a digital asset exchange designed to make buying, selling, and converting cryptocurrency straightforward — for individuals and businesses alike.
about.storyP1: We started GbitX because crypto in Africa was harder than it needed to be. Unreliable platforms, poor rates, and slow support were the norm. We built something different — a platform where transactions are fast, rates are competitive, and users always know exactly what they're getting.
about.storyP2: Today, GbitX serves individuals and businesses across the continent, offering a clean interface, mobile money and bank transfer support, and a team that actually responds when something goes wrong.
about.missionStatement: To make digital assets accessible, transparent, and part of everyday financial life for people across Africa and beyond.
about.valueSecureText: Non-custodial transactions, encrypted data, and no pooled user funds.
about.valueFastText: Orders confirmed quickly. Rates locked at execution. No waiting around.
about.valueSimpleText: Designed for first-time buyers and seasoned traders in equal measure.

giftcard.title: Gift Cards
giftcard.subtitle: Trade gift cards for crypto
giftcard.headerSubtitle: Sell all your valid Gift cards for GHS or USDT on GbitX...
giftcard.headerInfo: After calculating your gift card value and clicking 'Sell Now', you will be redirected to our verified agent on WhatsApp to complete your transaction.

business.title: Accept Crypto Payments
business.subtitle: Easy crypto payments for your business
business.heroTitle: Accept crypto payments from anywhere in the world.
business.heroSub: A powerful API that lets your platform receive crypto payments from any wallet, any exchange. Your users don't need a GbitX account. Built for developers and tech companies who want reliability and simplicity.
business.gatewayApiBody1: A clean, well-documented API that lets your platform receive crypto payments from anyone in the world. Your end users don't need a GbitX account. They can pay from any wallet, any exchange.
business.gatewayApiBody2: Every payment gets a unique blockchain address. You receive a webhook the moment a payment is confirmed. Built for developers who want reliability and simplicity without compromise.
business.feat1: Unique address generated per payment session
business.featWebhookRetry: Webhook delivery with retry and delivery logs
business.featSandbox: Sandbox mode for full integration testing
business.featSdks: SDKs for Node.js, Python, PHP and React Native
business.featDashboard: Full API dashboard with transaction and balance views
business.bottomSub: Create a developer account, get your API keys, and start accepting payments in minutes with our sandbox environment.
business.acceptCryptoTitle: Accept crypto payments with the GbitX Gateway.
business.acceptCryptoSub: Building a crypto-powered app or platform? GbitX has the API infrastructure to let you accept payments from any wallet, anywhere in the world.
business.integrateTitle: Integrate crypto payments into your product.
business.integrateSub: A clean REST API that lets your platform accept crypto from any wallet, anywhere in the world. Your users don't need a GbitX account. Webhook-driven, sandbox-ready, and built to scale.
business.feat4: SDKs for JavaScript, Python, PHP and more

docs.title: GbitX Pay API
docs.intro: Accept crypto payments from customers worldwide. Three ways to integrate: redirect to our hosted page, embed with the JavaScript SDK for an inline modal, or open the payment page in a WebView from your mobile app. All three deliver the same signed webhook to your backend on confirmation.

step1.title: Get API keys
step1.text: Create sandbox and live API keys from the API Keys tab in your dashboard.

step2.title: Create a payment
step2.text: POST to /v1/payments with the amount and currency. You get back a payment URL.

step3.title: Redirect customer
step3.text: Send the customer to the payment URL. They select a coin and pay.

step4.title: Receive webhook
step4.text: We notify your server when the payment is confirmed, expired, or underpaid.

ep.getPayment: Retrieve a single payment by ID.
ep.listPayments: List your payments with optional filters.
ep.getCoins: Get all supported cryptocurrencies and their networks.
ep.getRates: Get live crypto prices in any supported fiat currency.

docs.authIntro: All API requests require an API key sent in the Authorization header. Create keys from the API Keys tab in your developer dashboard.
docs.clockSync: Make sure your server's clock is synchronized via NTP. If your server's clock drifts by more than a few minutes, legitimate webhooks will be rejected as stale.
docs.embedChange1: The page does not auto-redirect on confirmed/cancelled — you control next steps.
docs.embedChange2: It emits postMessage events on terminal status changes (only when parentOrigin is also set).
docs.embedChange3: "Return to merchant" links become a "Close" button that emits a gbitx:payment:closed event.
docs.embedEventsIntro: The iframe emits messages to window.parent via postMessage. Always verify the event origin equals {{origin}} before trusting any event — otherwise a malicious script in your page could fake them.
docs.errorsIntro: All error responses follow a consistent format:

event.failed: Payment failed due to a system error.
event.loaded: Page mounted and ready (use to dismiss your loader).

docs.gkLive: gk_live_* — Live mode. Real blockchain transactions. Use in production.
docs.gkTest: gk_test_* — Sandbox mode. No real crypto involved. Use for development and testing.

http.400: Bad request — check your request body or parameters
http.401: Unauthorized — missing or invalid API key
http.403: Forbidden — your account is suspended or key is revoked
http.500: Server error — try again or contact support

docs.inlineIntro: Embed the GbitX payment page directly into your site as a modal or panel — no full-page redirect required. The customer never leaves your domain.
docs.keepKeysSecret: Keep your keys secret. Never expose API keys in client-side code, public repositories, or browser requests. If a key is compromised, revoke it immediately from the dashboard and create a new one.
docs.keyLimitsBody: Maximum 10 active API keys per merchant account. Sandbox and live keys are counted together.

lifecycle.awaiting: Coin selected. Deposit address assigned. Waiting for customer to send crypto.
lifecycle.confirmed: Payment confirmed on-chain. Funds credited to your merchant wallet.
lifecycle.confirming: Correct amount received. Waiting for blockchain confirmations.
lifecycle.expired: Payment expired before the customer paid (60-minute window).
lifecycle.failed: Payment failed due to a system error.
lifecycle.pending: Payment created. Customer hasn't selected a coin yet.
lifecycle.underpaid: Customer sent less than the required amount.

docs.mobileIntro: Native mobile apps integrate by embedding the payment page in a WebView and intercepting the redirect URL on completion. No native SDK is required — works on iOS, Android, React Native, and Flutter.
docs.mobileStep1: Your app's backend calls POST /v1/payments and returns the paymentUrl to the app.
docs.mobileStep2: The app opens the URL in a WebView (full screen, no ?embed=1 needed).
docs.mobileStep3: The WebView intercepts navigation. When the URL matches your redirectUrl or cancelUrl, dismiss the WebView and route in-app.
docs.mobileStep4: Your backend receives the signed webhook independently — it's the source of truth for fulfilment.
docs.mobileTip: Tip: Use unique redirectUrl / cancelUrl values per app version (e.g., https://yourapp.com/payment/success?v=1) so old app versions don't accidentally intercept newer flows.

opt.cancelToken: Used with paymentId. Without it, the customer cannot cancel from inside the modal.
opt.onClose: Fires whenever the modal goes away. reason is one of: 'closed' (in-iframe Close button), 'escape' (Esc key), 'backdrop' (clicked outside), 'programmatic' (you called handle.close()).
opt.onLoaded: Fires once when the iframe is ready. Use it to hide your loading spinner.
opt.onSuccess: Fires on CONFIRMED. Receives the public payment object (no address, no cancel token).
opt.paymentId: Alternative to paymentUrl. Pair with cancelToken if you want the in-modal cancel button to work.
opt.paymentUrl: The full paymentUrl from the response of POST /v1/payments. Includes the cancel token. Either this or paymentId must be provided.

params.amount: Payment amount in the specified currency. Maximum 1,000,000.
params.cancelUrl: URL to redirect the customer if they cancel. Same scheme restrictions as redirectUrl.
params.currency: Three-letter ISO currency code. Defaults to USD. Supported: USD, EUR, GBP, GHS, NGN, KES, ZAR, and 60+ more.
params.description: Description shown to the customer on the payment page. Max 255 characters.
params.externalRef: Your internal reference ID (e.g., order ID). Max 100 characters.
params.filterMode: Filter by mode: SANDBOX or LIVE.
params.filterStatus: Filter by status: PENDING, AWAITING_PAYMENT, CONFIRMING, CONFIRMED, UNDERPAID, EXPIRED, CANCELLED, FAILED.
params.idempotencyKey: Set this to a unique value (e.g., your order ID) to make the request safely retryable. If a payment with the same key has already been created for your account, we return that payment instead of creating a new one — even if the original response was lost in transit. Recommended for any client that automatically retries POST on connection errors. Max 255 printable ASCII characters, no whitespace. Scoped per-merchant.
params.metadata: Arbitrary key-value pairs stored with the payment. Returned in webhooks and API responses. Total JSON-serialized size limit: 4096 bytes.
params.offset: Number of results to skip. Default 0.
params.redirectUrl: URL to redirect the customer after successful payment. Must use https:// in production. http://localhost and http://127.0.0.1 are accepted for local development. javascript:, data:, and other browser-special schemes are rejected.

docs.parentOriginRequired: parentOrigin is required. Without it the page silently emits no postMessage events. This prevents random third parties from iframing arbitrary payment IDs to harvest event telemetry. The value must be the URL-encoded origin of the page hosting the iframe (https only — except http://localhost for development).
docs.payloadShapeIntro: The payment object on each event omits sensitive fields: the deposit address, the cancelToken, your private externalRef, and the on-chain txHash. Those are intentionally not exposed to the parent window. Read them from the signed webhook your backend receives.
docs.postman2Text: Go to File → Import → Raw Text, paste and import.
docs.postman3Text: Edit the collection variables and add your test API key.
docs.postmanIntro: Import this collection into Postman or Thunder Client to test all API endpoints instantly.
docs.publicEndpointsIntro: These public endpoints don't require authentication.
docs.rateLimitsIntro: Rate limits protect the API from abuse. Limits are applied per merchant or per IP address. When rate limited, you'll receive a 429 response.
docs.rawBodyImportant: Important: Use the raw request body for signature verification, not a parsed/re-serialized version. JSON re-serialization can change whitespace or key order, breaking the signature.
docs.rawIframeIntro: After creating a payment, drop the paymentUrl into an iframe. Add ?embed=1 to enable postMessage mode and ?parentOrigin=<your-site-origin> to tell the page where to deliver events.

req.deliveryLogs: You can view delivery logs in the Webhooks tab of your dashboard.
req.https: Your webhook URL must use HTTPS.

docs.retryIntro: If your server doesn't respond with a 2xx status, we retry up to 5 times with increasing delays:
docs.rlHeadersIntro: Rate limit headers are included in every response:
docs.sdkIntro: The drop-in checkout. Six lines on the merchant's page = a fully-styled, mobile-responsive payment modal with focus trap, escape-to-close, and event callbacks.
docs.sdkReturnsHandle: Returns a handle {{handle}} for programmatically dismissing the modal.
docs.tValueExplain: The t value is the unix timestamp (in seconds) at which we sent this delivery attempt — every retry generates a fresh timestamp, so legitimate retries hours later still pass verification. v1 is the HMAC-SHA256 of {{tpl}} using your webhook secret.
docs.useSdkInstead: For most merchants, use the JavaScript SDK below instead. It handles the iframe modal, the parentOrigin parameter, focus management, escape-to-close, and postMessage origin verification so you don't have to. The raw iframe approach is for advanced integrations only.
docs.verifyCheck1: Signature: v1 is the HMAC-SHA256 of {{tpl}} using your webhook secret as the key — this proves the request came from GbitX and the body wasn't tampered with.
docs.verifyCheck2: Freshness: t must be within ±5 minutes of your server's current time — this defeats replay attacks where an attacker captures a webhook and tries to re-send it later.
docs.verifyIntro: Always verify the X-GbitX-Signature header before trusting a webhook. The header has the format t=<unix_ts>,v1=<hmac_hex>. Verification has two independent checks, and both must pass:
docs.versioningBody: The SDK is served from {{url}} with a 5-minute browser cache and 24-hour CDN cache. Bug fixes and non-breaking improvements roll out automatically. Future breaking changes will ship at /v2/checkout.js and the v1 URL will continue to work indefinitely.
docs.webhooksConfigure: Webhooks notify your server in real-time when a payment status changes. Configure your webhook URL in the Webhooks tab of your dashboard. You'll receive a secret — save it to verify webhook signatures.
docs.webhooksSourceTruthEmbed: Webhooks remain the source of truth. Use onSuccess / postMessage events to update your UI, but never mark an order as paid in your database based on the JS event alone — a malicious customer can fake it from devtools. Mark orders paid only after verifying the signed webhook on your backend.
docs.webhooksSourceTruthMain: Webhooks are your source of truth. No matter which integration you use — REST, inline checkout, JavaScript SDK, or WebView — your backend should mark orders as paid only after verifying a signed webhook from us. Browser callbacks and redirect URLs run on the customer's device and can be tampered with.
docs.webhooksSourceTruthMobile: Webhooks are the source of truth. The redirect URL fires from the customer's device — it can be tampered with. Always confirm payment status on your backend via the signed webhook before fulfilling the order.
docs.webhooksSourceTruthSdk: Webhooks are the source of truth. The onSuccess callback is for UX — closing the modal, updating the cart, navigating to a thank-you page. Never mark an order as paid in your database based on it alone. A malicious customer can fake this event from their browser console. Verify the signed webhook on your backend before fulfilling.

wh.expired: Payment expired with no payment received.
wh.expiredUnderpaid: Payment expired but partial funds were received.
wh.pending: Customer selected a coin. Address assigned.
wh.underpaid: Amount received is less than required.

docs.whatToDoResponse: What to do with the response: Redirect your customer to paymentUrl. They will select a cryptocurrency, see the deposit address, and send the payment. The payment expires after 60 minutes.
docs.whyIdempotency: Why use it: if your HTTP client retries a POST after a network blip, without an idempotency key you get two payment rows for one logical order. With the key, the retry returns the original payment.

paymentLinks.intro: Payment Links let you accept crypto from customers without writing any code. Create a link in the dashboard, share the URL — by email, on social media, in an invoice PDF, or as a QR code — and the customer pays at pay.gbitx.com. Every link is backed by the same hosted checkout, the same webhook stack, and the same fee schedule as a direct API payment.
paymentLinks.singleUseBody: Set maxUses to 1. The link works once; after a customer pays (or even just opens the checkout) it stops accepting new sessions. Use these for invoices addressed to a specific customer, or any payment you don't want shared.
paymentLinks.reusableBody: Leave maxUses unset (or set a higher cap like 100). The link can be paid any number of times. Each click mints a fresh Payment row. Ideal for product pages, donation buttons, and tip jars.
paymentLinks.modesBody: Set amountMode to FIXED when you know exactly what to charge (a $29.99 subscription, a 0.05 BTC invoice). Set it to CUSTOMER_ENTERS when you don't — the landing page shows a number input with optional min/max bounds. Useful for donations, top-ups, and pay-what-you-want.
paymentLinks.urlBody: Every link is identified by a cryptographically random 16-character code. The URL you share with customers looks like:
paymentLinks.urlSecurity: The code carries 80 bits of entropy — enumeration by guessing is computationally infeasible. We additionally rate-limit lookups by IP and 404 every non-live state (inactive / expired / over-cap / merchant suspended) with the same response so an attacker can't distinguish a real-but-disabled link from a non-existent one.
paymentLinks.apiBody: You can manage links from the dashboard OR via the merchant API. The dashboard UI is just a thin wrapper around these endpoints. Auth is the same as every other merchant-side endpoint — your session cookie.
paymentLinks.flowBody: A customer clicks your share URL. The public landing renders the link title, description, image, and either the fixed amount or an input. They press Pay — that mints a Payment row (with a paymentLinkId pointing back to your link) and bounces them to the standard hosted checkout. From there the existing payment lifecycle takes over: coin selection, deposit address + QR, confirmations, and the same set of webhook events you see on direct-API payments. The customer never knows they came in through a link.
paymentLinks.calloutBody: Every Payment created from a link carries paymentLinkId on the row + paymentLinkCode in metadata. Both surface in the webhook payload so you can split confirmations by source link without making an extra API call.

ep.create: Create a new payment link. Returns the link + a shareUrl ready to copy.
ep.list: List your links. Cursor-paginated; filter by ?mode= and ?active=.
ep.detail: Link detail + usage stats (total, confirmed, in-flight, USD volume) + 10 most-recent payments minted from this link.
ep.patch: Edit non-immutable fields: title, description, active, maxUses, expiresAt, success/cancelUrl, amount bounds, metadata. amountMode and mode cannot be changed — create a new link instead.
ep.delete: Soft-delete. The link 404s for customers immediately; historical payments minted from it remain intact in your dashboard.

params.title: Up to 120 chars. Shown at the top of the public landing page (e.g. "Pro plan — monthly").
params.description: Optional. Up to 500 chars. Shown under the title.
params.imageUrl: Optional. https:// only. Renders above the title on the landing page (max 200 px tall).
params.mode: 'SANDBOX' or 'LIVE'. LIVE requires your merchant account to be APPROVED.
params.amountMode: 'FIXED' (you set the amount) or 'CUSTOMER_ENTERS' (customer types it on the landing).
params.currency: ISO 4217 code (e.g. USD, EUR). Required when amountMode = FIXED. Optional restriction when CUSTOMER_ENTERS — null lets the customer pick.
params.minAmount: Optional bound for amountMode = CUSTOMER_ENTERS. Enforced server-side at checkout.
params.maxAmount: Optional bound for amountMode = CUSTOMER_ENTERS. Enforced server-side at checkout.
params.maxUses: Optional. Caps how many payments the link can mint. Leave null for unlimited. Set to 1 for a single-use invoice link.
params.expiresAt: Optional. Link returns 404 to customers after this timestamp regardless of usedCount.
params.successUrl: Optional. Where to send the customer after a confirmed payment. https:// only.
params.cancelUrl: Optional. Where to send the customer if they cancel on the checkout.
params.metadata: Optional JSON object. Copied into every Payment row + every webhook payload as paymentLinkCode + your fields, so you can correlate confirmations back to the source link. Max 4 KB.

gate.title: Verification Required
gate.text: You need to complete Level 2 (ID Verification) before you can access the Developer Portal. Your current verification level is Level {{level}}.
gate.subtext: L2 verification confirms your identity and unlocks access to the payment gateway, API keys, and developer tools.

pending.rejectedBody: Unfortunately, your application didn't meet our current requirements.
pending.reapplyBody: You may update your details and resubmit for review.
pending.underReviewBody: Thank you for applying! Our team is reviewing your business details. This typically takes 1–2 business days.

onboarding.gatewayIntro: Accept crypto payments from anywhere in the world. A powerful API that lets your platform receive crypto payments from any wallet, any exchange. Your users don't need a GbitX account. Built for developers and tech companies who want reliability and simplicity.
onboarding.title: Business Application
onboarding.subtitle: Tell us about your business to get started with crypto payments
onboarding.currenciesPrompt: Which currencies will your customers pay in? *
onboarding.legalNote: By submitting, you agree that all information provided is accurate. Your application will be reviewed within 1–2 business days.
onboarding.errorRequired: Please fill in all required fields.

payment.notFound: Payment not found or has expired.
payment.testModeBanner: Test mode — no real crypto will be sent or received
payment.overpaidBanner: You sent {{amount}} {{crypto}} more than required. The merchant has received your full payment.
payment.cancelTokenMissing: Cancel is only available from the original payment link. Please reopen the link your merchant gave you.
payment.failedAssignAddress: Failed to assign address. Try again.
payment.simulateRequiresLink: Simulate requires the original payment link.

warning.network: Ensure you are using the correct network.
warning.networkLoss: Using the incorrect network may result in irreversible loss of funds.
warning.asset: Make sure you use the correct asset for this payment.
warning.singleUse: This address is for this payment only. Do not save it for future use.
warning.fees: Your wallet or exchange may charge a fee. Send the exact amount shown to avoid underpayment.
warning.timing: Send before the timer expires. Exchange withdrawals can take 15 to 30 minutes, so start the send early. If your payment arrives late, contact the merchant directly with your transaction ID.
